GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) performing a landing.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program aims to develop all the necessary technologies for a fully reusable orbital launch vehicle.

GS3.headline: India's Q1 FY25 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations, Driven by Manufacturing and Services Sector
GS3.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) registered a robust growth of 7.8% in the first quarter (April-June) of fiscal year 2024-25, surpassing market expectations.
• The strong performance was primarily attributed to sustained momentum in the manufacturing sector and a rebound in services, particularly trade, hotels, and transport.
• Private consumption expenditure showed encouraging signs of recovery, while government capital expenditure continued to provide support.
• Analysts highlight the resilience of the Indian economy amidst global headwinds and domestic policy support.
• This growth trajectory reinforces India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Growth, Development and Employment.

GS3.imageDescription: A graph showing upward trending economic growth with Indian Rupee symbols.
GS3.trivia: India's GDP is the fifth-largest in the world by nominal terms and third-largest by purchasing power parity (PPP).

GS3.headline: Indian Army Conducts 'Exercise Vajra Prahar' with US Special Forces in Himachal Pradesh
GS3.explainer:
• The 14th edition of the joint military exercise 'Vajra Prahar' between the Indian Army's Special Forces and US Special Forces commenced in Bakloh, Himachal Pradesh.
• The exercise focuses on interoperability in counter-terrorism operations, special operations, and airborne assaults in mountainous terrain.
• It involves detailed planning, tactical drills, and live-fire exercises, enhancing the combat readiness and coordination between the two forces.
• 'Vajra Prahar' is a crucial component of the growing defense cooperation between India and the United States.
• The exercise aims to share best practices, improve joint command and control structures, and strengthen strategic partnership.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas; linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.

Impact on regional security dynamics and India's strategic autonomy.
GS3.imageDescription: Soldiers from Indian and US Special Forces during a joint exercise.
GS3.trivia: The 'Vajra Prahar' series of exercises alternates between India and the United States annually.

GS3.headline: India Develops Indigenous 5G Testbed for Rural Broadband Deployment, Bridging Digital Divide
GS3.explainer:
• Indian researchers and telecom companies have successfully developed an indigenous 5G testbed specifically designed for rural broadband deployment, announced the Department of Telecommunications.
• The testbed aims to provide affordable and reliable 5G connectivity in remote and underserved areas, leveraging local innovations.
• It integrates features like low-power consumption, extended range, and robust security protocols suitable for challenging rural environments.
• This initiative is critical for bridging India's digital divide and promoting digital inclusion under the 'Digital India' mission.
• The deployment of this technology is expected to boost e-governance, digital education, and telemedicine services in rural India.

Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.

GS3.trivia: India aims to have 100% broadband connectivity in all villages by 2025 under the BharatNet project.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing to Boost EV Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government introduced a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing in India.
• This scheme aims to attract significant investments in the indigenous production of high-efficiency batteries, crucial for the electric vehicle (EV) industry and grid-scale energy storage.
• It offers incentives based on sales of manufactured goods, promoting economies of scale and global competitiveness.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to reduce import dependence, enhance domestic manufacturing capabilities, and accelerate the transition to sustainable mobility.
• This PLI scheme is expected to create a robust ecosystem for battery manufacturing, fostering innovation and job creation in the sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.

GS3.trivia: India's target is to achieve 30% EV penetration in private cars, 70% in commercial vehicles, and 80% in two-wheelers and three-wheelers by 2030.

GS3.headline: India's First Private Sector Rocket Engine for Space Missions Successfully Test-Fired
GS3.explainer:
• A leading Indian space-tech startup successfully test-fired its indigenously developed liquid-fueled rocket engine, marking a significant milestone for India's private space sector.
• This is the first time a private Indian company has developed and tested a fully functional rocket engine capable of powering orbital launch vehicles.
• The engine is designed for efficiency and reliability, aiming to provide cost-effective propulsion solutions for small satellite launches.
• The development aligns with the government's space reforms, which encourage private sector participation in space exploration and commercialization.
• This achievement is set to boost India's capabilities in the global space launch market and foster a vibrant private space ecosystem.

GS3.trivia: India's space economy is projected to grow significantly, with private sector contributions expected to play a major role in achieving this growth.

GS3.headline: Parliament Passes National Research Foundation Bill, Paving Way for Enhanced R&D Funding
GS3.explainer:
• Parliament passed the National Research Foundation (NRF) Bill, 2024, establishing an apex body to foster a strong research and development (R&D) ecosystem across India.
• The NRF will be an autonomous body tasked with synergizing research efforts, funding scientific and technological innovations, and promoting industry-academia linkages.
• It aims to substantially increase private sector investment in R&D and ensure equitable funding distribution across various disciplines and institutions.
• The foundation will prioritize research in emerging areas like AI, quantum computing, biotechnology, and sustainable technologies.
• This legislative step is crucial for strengthening India's scientific capabilities and achieving global leadership in innovation.
